entries.description: The main entry point for the React application. It finds the 'root' DOM element and renders the main 'App' component into it.
Contains metadata for the LumenCRM application, including its name and a brief description. No special frame permissions are requested.

The main HTML file for the application. It sets up the page structure, loads Google Fonts (Inter), configures Tailwind CSS with a custom theme (colors, fonts), and uses an import map to manage modern JavaScript module imports for React, Recharts, and Lucide-React.

The root component of the application. It manages the main layout, including the Sidebar and the main content area. It uses a state variable 'currentView' to handle simple client-side routing, rendering different components based on the user's navigation choices.

This file defines shared TypeScript types and enums used across the application. It includes the 'View' enum for navigation and the 'User' interface for user data structure, ensuring type safety and consistency.

A centralized module for managing and exporting icons from the 'lucide-react' library. It creates an 'Icons' object to provide easy access to specific icons throughout the application, and also defines the main SVG 'Logo' component.

The main navigation sidebar component. It displays the company logo, a list of navigable sections with icons, and user account information at the bottom. It highlights the currently active view and allows users to switch between different pages of the application.
A generic placeholder component used for pages that are still under construction. It displays a title and a simple message to inform the user that the content is not yet available.
A simple, reusable Card component that provides a consistent container style with a dark background, padding, and rounded corners. It's used throughout the application to wrap sections of content.

A highly reusable Button component with support for different visual variants ('primary', 'secondary', 'ghost', 'success'). It standardizes button styling and behavior, including hover effects and disabled states, across the application.

A reusable toggle switch component that provides a boolean on/off input. It is styled to match the application's theme and handles its own state, with an optional 'onChange' callback to notify parent components of state changes.

The main Dashboard component. It displays key CRM metrics using 'StatCard' components and visualizes data with a PieChart for negotiation statuses and a BarChart for negotiations by stage, utilizing the 'recharts' library.

The Settings page component, which focuses on user management. It displays a list of users in a table, allowing for status changes via a toggle switch. It also includes a modal for editing detailed user permissions, which is triggered by an edit button.

A reusable modal dialog component. It renders a semi-transparent overlay and a centered content box. It's controlled by an 'isOpen' prop and includes a title and a close button, making it easy to display overlay content anywhere in the app.

This component builds the chat interface of the CRM. It features a two-column layout: a sidebar for listing active conversations and a main area for displaying the message history of the selected chat, complete with message bubbles and a message input form.

A reusable styled text input component. It applies consistent theming (background, border, text color, focus ring) to standard HTML input elements, ensuring a uniform look and feel for all text fields in the application.

This component provides the interface for managing and testing the AI Agent. It uses a tabbed layout to switch between different sections like 'Agent Settings', 'Follow-up', and 'Agent Test'. The test tab includes a mock chat interface for interacting with the agent.

The Sales Automation module. It provides a two-part interface: a list view displaying existing workflows on cards, and a detailed workflow builder view for creating or editing automation sequences. The builder is highly interactive, featuring a visual representation of steps and side panels for adding and configuring actions.
